title: Iran Conflict Tracker Update — Fri Apr 24, 2026
summary: On Day 56, the USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) entered the CENTCOM AOR, making three US carriers simultaneously in the Middle East for the first time in 20+ years, accompanied by Marines and destroyers. title: Iran Conflict Tracker Update — Thu Apr 23, 2026
summary: On Day 55, the US Navy seized the M/T Majestic X in the Indian Ocean — the third vessel detained under the Iran blockade in four days — as the dual-blockade standoff deepened. The Senate rejected Democrats' fifth war powers challenge (51-46). Peace negotiations remain stalled with Iran refusing Islamabad Round 2.

title: Iran Conflict Tracker Update — Wed Apr 22, 2026
summary: On Day 54, Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely — citing Iran's 'seriously fractured' government — but kept the naval blockade fully in force and VP Vance canceled his Islamabad trip as Iran refused to send a delegation. Hours after the extension, Iran's IRGC Navy retaliated by seizing cargo ships MSC Francesca and Epaminondas in the Strait of Hormuz and firing on a third vessel, the Euphoria, which ran aground. Brent crude spiked toward $99/bbl near $100 before pulling back in volatile trading. US officials disclosed an 'absolute fracture' inside Iran, with IRGC hardliners under Gen. Vahidi blocking civilian negotiators from reaching the supreme leader.

title: Iran Conflict Tracker Update — Tue Apr 21, 2026
summary: On Day 53, VP JD Vance, Special Envoy Witkoff, and Jared Kushner departed for Islamabad to lead Round 2 talks as the ceasefire counts down to its April 22 expiration, but Iran's Foreign Ministry dismissed the delegation's trip as 'a media game' and declined to confirm participation. Trump declared a ceasefire extension 'highly unlikely' without a deal and reiterated threats to destroy all Iranian power plants and bridges. Brent crude dipped slightly to ~$94.78/bbl on thin optimism that Iran might yet send a delegation, while the US Navy turned back 21 commercial ships in Arabian Sea blockade enforcement. Xi Jinping called Saudi Crown Prince MBS urging the Strait of Hormuz to stay open, and Iran's Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf warned Tehran is 'prepared to reveal new cards on the battlefield.'

Oil prices surged sharply — Brent +6.3% to ~$96/bbl, WTI +7% to ~$88.3/bbl — as ceasefire expiration looms April 22 with no deal in sight.
